list.name: user
list.query: SELECT id AS __value, display_name AS __text FROM users ORDER BY display_name
list.label: Athlete

list.name: exercise
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT exercise_name AS __value, exercise_name AS __text FROM strength_sets WHERE user_id = $user ORDER BY exercise_name
list.label: Exercise

panels.title: Workouts This Week

targets.rawSql: SET LOCAL timezone = '$tz'; SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT time::date) AS "Sessions" FROM strength_sets WHERE user_id = $user AND time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'

panels.description: Number of training sessions this week. Aim for 3-5 sessions per week for optimal results.

panels.title: Total Volume This Week

targets.rawSql: SET LOCAL timezone = '$tz'; SELECT time_bucket('1 week', time) AS time, ROUND(SUM(COALESCE(weight_kg,0) * COALESCE(reps,0))::numeric, 0) AS "Volume" FROM strength_sets WHERE user_id = $user AND set_type = 'normal' AND time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '4 weeks' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.description: Total weight moved (weight × reps) this week. The sparkline shows your last 4 weeks — look for an upward trend.

panels.title: Sets This Week

targets.rawSql: SET LOCAL timezone = '$tz'; SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Sets" FROM strength_sets WHERE user_id = $user AND set_type = 'normal' AND time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'

panels.description: Total working sets this week. 15-25 sets per session is a good range for most people.

panels.title: Body Map — This Week

targets.rawSql: SET LOCAL timezone = '$tz'; SELECT COALESCE(NULLIF(muscle_group,''),'other') AS muscle, COUNT(*) AS sets FROM strength_sets WHERE user_id = $user AND set_type = 'normal' AND time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days' GROUP BY 1

panels.description: Visual heatmap of your body showing which muscles you trained this week. Brighter colors mean more sets. Dark areas are muscles you have not hit recently.

panels.title: Sets Per Muscle Group
panels.description: Sets per muscle group in the selected time range. Optimal weekly volume for hypertrophy is 10-20 sets per major muscle. Red = undertrained, Green = on target, Blue = high volume.

targets.rawSql:
WITH muscle_order AS (
  SELECT unnest(ARRAY['shoulders','chest','upper_back','lats','biceps','triceps',
    'abdominals','quadriceps','glutes','hamstrings','calves']) AS mg,
  generate_series(1,11) AS ord
),
counts AS (
  SELECT muscle_group, COUNT(*) AS sets
  FROM strength_sets
  WHERE user_id = $user AND set_type = 'normal' AND $__timeFilter(time)
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT
  MAX(CASE WHEN mo.mg='shoulders'  THEN COALESCE(c.sets,0) END) AS "Shoulders",
  MAX(CASE WHEN mo.mg='chest'      THEN COALESCE(c.sets,0) END) AS "Chest",
  MAX(CASE WHEN mo.mg='upper_back' THEN COALESCE(c.sets,0) END) AS "Upper Back",
  MAX(CASE WHEN mo.mg='lats'       THEN COALESCE(c.sets,0) END) AS "Lats",
  MAX(CASE WHEN mo.mg='biceps'     THEN COALESCE(c.sets,0) END) AS "Biceps",
  MAX(CASE WHEN mo.mg='triceps'    THEN COALESCE(c.sets,0) END) AS "Triceps",
  MAX(CASE WHEN mo.mg='abdominals' THEN COALESCE(c.sets,0) END) AS "Core",
  MAX(CASE WHEN mo.mg='quadriceps' THEN COALESCE(c.sets,0) END) AS "Quads",
  MAX(CASE WHEN mo.mg='glutes'     THEN COALESCE(c.sets,0) END) AS "Glutes",
  MAX(CASE WHEN mo.mg='hamstrings' THEN COALESCE(c.sets,0) END) AS "Hamstrings",
  MAX(CASE WHEN mo.mg='calves'     THEN COALESCE(c.sets,0) END) AS "Calves"
FROM muscle_order mo
LEFT JOIN counts c ON c.muscle_group = mo.mg

panels.title: Training Balance (Push / Pull / Legs)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE muscle_group IN ('chest','shoulders','triceps')) AS "Push",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE muscle_group IN ('upper_back','lats','biceps')) AS "Pull",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE muscle_group IN ('quadriceps','hamstrings','glutes','calves')) AS "Legs",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE muscle_group NOT IN ('chest','shoulders','triceps','upper_back','lats','biceps','quadriceps','hamstrings','glutes','calves') AND muscle_group != '') AS "Other"
FROM strength_sets
WHERE user_id = $user AND set_type = 'normal' AND $__timeFilter(time)

panels.description: Balance between movement patterns. Aim for roughly equal distribution. Large imbalances increase injury risk over time.

panels.title: Days Since Last Trained
panels.description: How long since each muscle was last trained. Muscles not hit in 7+ days need attention. Beyond 14 days you start losing adaptations.

targets.rawSql:
SET LOCAL timezone = '$tz'; SELECT
  CASE COALESCE(NULLIF(muscle_group,''),'other')
    WHEN 'shoulders' THEN 'Shoulders'
    WHEN 'chest' THEN 'Chest'
    WHEN 'upper_back' THEN 'Upper Back'
    WHEN 'lats' THEN 'Lats'
    WHEN 'biceps' THEN 'Biceps'
    WHEN 'triceps' THEN 'Triceps'
    WHEN 'abdominals' THEN 'Core'
    WHEN 'quadriceps' THEN 'Quads'
    WHEN 'glutes' THEN 'Glutes'
    WHEN 'hamstrings' THEN 'Hamstrings'
    WHEN 'calves' THEN 'Calves'
    WHEN 'full_body' THEN 'Full Body'
    WHEN 'cardio' THEN 'Cardio'
    ELSE INITCAP(REPLACE(COALESCE(NULLIF(muscle_group,''),'other'), '_', ' '))
  END AS "Muscle",
  TO_CHAR(MAX(time::date), 'Mon DD') AS "Last Trained",
  (CURRENT_DATE - MAX(time::date)) AS "Days Ago"
FROM strength_sets
WHERE user_id = $user AND set_type = 'normal'
GROUP BY muscle_group
ORDER BY 3 DESC

panels.title: Training Frequency
panels.description: Number of training days per week. 3-5 days/week is ideal for most people. More than 6 risks overtraining without adequate recovery.

targets.rawSql: SET LOCAL timezone = '$tz'; SELECT time_bucket('1 week', time) AS time, COUNT(DISTINCT time::date) AS "Sessions" FROM strength_sets WHERE user_id = $user AND $__timeFilter(time) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Weekly Volume Trend
panels.description: Total weight moved per week (weight × reps). A gradual upward trend means progressive overload is working. Sudden drops may signal fatigue or a planned deload.

targets.rawSql: SET LOCAL timezone = '$tz'; SELECT time_bucket('1 week', time) AS time, ROUND(SUM(COALESCE(weight_kg,0) * COALESCE(reps,0))::numeric, 0) AS "Volume (kg)" FROM strength_sets WHERE user_id = $user AND set_type = 'normal' AND weight_kg > 0 AND $__timeFilter(time) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Estimated 1RM Trend
panels.description: Estimated one-rep max using the Epley formula (weight × (1 + reps/30)). A rising line means you are building strength. Flat or declining lines may mean it is time to change your program.

targets.rawSql: SET LOCAL timezone = '$tz'; WITH ranked AS ( SELECT time::date AS day, exercise_name, weight_kg * (1 + reps / 30.0) AS est_1rm, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY exercise_name, time::date ORDER BY weight_kg * (1 + reps / 30.0) DESC) AS rn FROM strength_sets WHERE user_id = $user AND set_type = 'normal' AND weight_kg > 0 AND reps > 0 AND reps <= 12 AND $__timeFilter(time) AND exercise_name IN ( SELECT exercise_name FROM strength_sets WHERE user_id = $user AND weight_kg > 0 AND time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '4 weeks' GROUP BY exercise_name ORDER BY MAX(weight_kg) DESC LIMIT 8 ) ) SELECT day AS time, exercise_name AS metric, ROUND(est_1rm::numeric, 1) AS value FROM ranked WHERE rn = 1 ORDER BY day

panels.title: Exercise Progress
panels.description: Select an exercise from the dropdown above to track your progression. Look for gradual increases in weight at the same rep ranges over time.

targets.rawSql: SELECT time, weight_kg AS "Weight (kg)", reps AS "Reps" FROM strength_sets WHERE user_id = $user AND set_type = 'normal' AND weight_kg IS NOT NULL AND exercise_name LIKE '$exercise' AND $__timeFilter(time) ORDER BY time

panels.title: Recent Personal Records
panels.description: New personal bests — celebrate these! Each row shows a day you estimated a higher 1RM than ever before on that exercise.

targets.rawSql: SET LOCAL timezone = '$tz'; WITH daily_best AS ( SELECT time::date AS day, exercise_name, weight_kg, reps, ROUND((weight_kg * (1 + reps / 30.0))::numeric, 1) AS est_1rm, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY exercise_name, time::date ORDER BY weight_kg * (1 + reps / 30.0) DESC) AS rn FROM strength_sets WHERE user_id = $user AND set_type = 'normal' AND weight_kg > 0 AND reps > 0 AND reps <= 12 ), bests AS ( SELECT day, exercise_name, weight_kg, reps, est_1rm, MAX(est_1rm) OVER (PARTITION BY exercise_name ORDER BY day ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING) AS prev_best FROM daily_best WHERE rn = 1 ) SELECT day AS "Date", exercise_name AS "Exercise", weight_kg AS "Weight (kg)", reps AS "Reps", est_1rm AS "Est 1RM (kg)", ROUND((est_1rm - prev_best)::numeric, 1) AS "Improvement (kg)" FROM bests WHERE est_1rm > COALESCE(prev_best, 0) ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 20

panels.title: Recent Workouts

targets.rawSql: SET LOCAL timezone = '$tz'; SELECT time::date AS "Date", COUNT(DISTINCT exercise_name) AS "Exercises", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE set_type = 'normal') AS "Working Sets", ROUND(SUM(COALESCE(weight_kg, 0) * COALESCE(reps, 0))::numeric, 0) AS "Total Volume (kg)", string_agg(DISTINCT COALESCE(NULLIF(muscle_group,''),'other'), ', ' ORDER BY COALESCE(NULLIF(muscle_group,''),'other')) AS "Muscles" FROM strength_sets WHERE user_id = $user AND $__timeFilter(time) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1 DESC LIMIT 20

panels.description: Your training log at a glance. Use this to verify your data and spot patterns in session structure.
